title: Fix worktree file-mode drift (664/775 on disk vs git-tracked 644/755) blocking the baseline verify
action: This worktree checkout left 477 tracked files at mode 664 (git tracks 100644) and 3 at mode 775 (git tracks 100755) -- pure OS-umask artifact from worktree creation, zero content change (git diff shows nothing; run_verify.mjs's release_manifest --check flags it as 480 MISMATCH: mode differ). Fix: for every path git ls-files -s reports as 100644, chmod 644; for every 100755, chmod 755. Do not touch content. This is a pre-existing environment artifact, not caused by any feature work in this session.

truths: Every git-tracked file's on-disk mode matches its git index mode (100644 -> 644, 100755 -> 755)
node scripts/run_verify.mjs shows zero MISMATCH lines and release_manifest --check passes
git status shows no content changes from this cell (mode-only fix, git itself does not track non-exec-bit permission changes so this produces no diff)
prohibitions: Do not modify any file's content

verify: git ls-files -s | while read mode hash stage path; do case "$mode" in 100644) want=644;; 100755) want=755;; *) continue;; esac; cur=$(stat -c %a "$path" 2>/dev/null); [ "$cur" = "$want" ] || echo "BAD $path $cur!=$want"; done | grep -q . && exit 1 || echo ALL_MODES_OK; node scripts/run_verify.mjs 2>&1 | tail -5

trace.dropped_reason: Pre-existing worktree file-mode drift caused by run_verify.mjs's own render step regenerating projected files under a group-write umask -- not caused by, or fixable within, this feature; fixing it requires the umask/render script itself, out of scope. User directed dropping this from the feature's cells.
